reasoning: The resolution criteria for this question ask whether OpenAI will claim GPT-5 is AGI within 30 days after its release (or before its release). To date (May 2025), GPT-5 has not yet been released, but recent tweets and statements by CEO Sam Altman in April 2025 indicated it is "a few months" away. More importantly, declaring a model to be AGI carries massive financial consequences due to OpenAI's contract with Microsoft, wherein Microsoft's IP rights exclude AGI models. Reports from late 2024 revealed that OpenAI and Microsoft defined AGI internally as an autonomous system that can generate $100 billion in profits. It is extremely unlikely that GPT-5 could generate $100 billion in profits within 30 days of release. While there's speculation OpenAI could declare AGI simply to sever Microsoft's connection, doing so at the GPT-5 milestone goes against recent communications where Altman tempers expectations, explicitly stating they are not there yet ("a lot of work to do"). Given the high bar (contractual, operational, and PR-related) for making an official AGI claim for a specific model immediately upon release, the likelihood of this occurring exactly for GPT-5 within the 30-day window is incredibly low, aligning with the market prediction of ~0.01. I assign a probability of 0.02.
